title: Google OAuth Auth System with Better Auth

tasks.description: Install Better Auth + nestjs-better-auth packages in backend and configure the auth instance with Google OAuth and Drizzle adapter
steps: Run `cd backend && pnpm add better-auth @thallesp/nestjs-better-auth drizzle-orm` to install all auth packages

Create `backend/src/auth/auth.ts` — export a `betterAuth()` instance configured with: socialProviders.google (clientId/clientSecret from ConfigService or process.env), drizzleAdapter(db, { provider: 'pg' }) using a new Drizzle instance from a pg Pool, trustedOrigins: [process.env.FRONTEND_URL || 'http://localhost:3000'], basePath: '/api/auth'
The Drizzle instance in auth.ts is ONLY for Better Auth — the rest of the backend continues using raw pg Pool queries
Add GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL to `backend/.env.example` and `backend/.env`

tasks.description: Generate Better Auth database schema and add it to the Drizzle schema file alongside existing agents table
steps: Run `cd backend && npx @better-auth/cli@latest generate --config ./src/auth/auth.ts --output ./generated-auth-schema.ts` to generate the Drizzle schema for user/session/account/verification tables
Read the generated file and merge the table definitions (user, session, account, verification) into `mastra/src/db/schema.ts` alongside the existing agents pgTable
Ensure the user table id is `text` type (Better Auth default for Postgres) — do NOT change it to uuid
Delete the generated temp file after merging
Run `cd mastra && pnpm run generate` to create migration SQL
Run `cd mastra && pnpm run migrate` to apply the migration and create the tables

tasks.description: Add user_id FK column to agents table so agents can be owned by users
steps: In `mastra/src/db/schema.ts`, add `userId: text('user_id').references(() => user.id)` to the agents table (nullable — existing agents have no owner)
Run `cd mastra && pnpm run generate` to create the ALTER TABLE migration SQL
Review the generated migration SQL to confirm it adds a nullable user_id column with FK reference to user.id
Run `cd mastra && pnpm run migrate` to apply

tasks.description: Mount Better Auth in NestJS via @thallesp/nestjs-better-auth — disable body parser, register AuthModule
steps: In `backend/src/main.ts`, add `bodyParser: false` to `NestFactory.create(AppModule, { bodyParser: false })` — this is CRITICAL, without it Better Auth requests hang because NestJS parses the body before Better Auth can
In `backend/src/app.module.ts`, import `AuthModule` from `@thallesp/nestjs-better-auth` and add `AuthModule.forRoot({ auth })` to the imports array, passing the auth instance from `./auth/auth.ts`
The nestjs-better-auth package automatically: mounts `/api/auth/*` routes, re-adds body parsers for non-auth routes, registers a global AuthGuard
Verify CORS config in main.ts still has `credentials: true` and includes the frontend origin

tasks.description: Mark public endpoints with @AllowAnonymous and add @Session() to protected controllers
steps: The nestjs-better-auth AuthGuard is registered globally — all routes are protected by default
In `backend/src/app.controller.ts`, add `@AllowAnonymous()` decorator to the health check endpoint
In `backend/src/agents/agents.controller.ts`, import `Session` and `UserSession` from `@thallesp/nestjs-better-auth`
Add `@Session() session: UserSession` parameter to each controller method that needs the user
Pass `session.user.id` to service methods as the userId parameter
In `backend/src/arena/arena.controller.ts`, add `@AllowAnonymous()` to the join endpoint (external agents join via invite token, not user auth)
In `backend/src/livekit/livekit.controller.ts`, use `@Session()` to get the user for token generation

tasks.description: Update AgentsService to filter and enforce ownership by user_id
steps: Update `AgentsService.listAgents()` to accept `userId: string` param and add `WHERE user_id = $N` condition. Also return agents where `user_id IS NULL` (legacy/seed agents visible to all)
Update `AgentsService.createAgent()` to include `user_id` in the INSERT statement, using the authenticated user's ID
Update `AgentsService.updateAgent()` to add `AND (user_id = $N OR user_id IS NULL)` to the WHERE clause — users can only edit their own agents or unowned ones
Update `AgentsService.deleteAgent()` to add `AND user_id = $N` — users can only delete their own agents (not seed/unowned ones)
Update `CreateAgentDto` — no changes needed since userId comes from session, not request body

tasks.description: Install Better Auth React client in the frontend and create the auth client module
steps: Run `cd ui-web && pnpm add better-auth`

Create `ui-web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`: import `createAuthClient` from `better-auth/react`, configure with `baseURL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:4200'` and `fetchOptions: { credentials: 'include' }`
Export `authClient`, `signIn`, `signOut`, `useSession` from the module
The `credentials: 'include'` in fetchOptions ensures auth cookies are sent with all Better Auth client requests

tasks.description: Add credentials: include to the API client so auth cookies are sent with every backend request
steps: In `ui-web/src/lib/api.ts`, add `credentials: 'include' as RequestCredentials` to the fetch init object in the `request()` method
This ensures the Better Auth session cookie is sent with all agent CRUD API calls, not just auth-specific calls

tasks.description: Restructure app directory with route groups — (auth) without NavBar, (app) with NavBar
steps: Create route group `ui-web/src/app/(auth)/` with its own `layout.tsx` — minimal layout, no NavBar/StatusBar, centered content
Move login page to `ui-web/src/app/(auth)/auth/login/page.tsx`
Create route group `ui-web/src/app/(app)/` with `layout.tsx` that includes NavBar, StatusBar, and LiveSessionProvider (moved from root layout)
Move existing pages into (app): `(app)/page.tsx` (dashboard), `(app)/agents/`, `(app)/arena/`, `(app)/live/`, `(app)/settings/`, `(app)/profile/`
Simplify `ui-web/src/app/layout.tsx` to only contain html/body wrapper with fonts and globals.css — no NavBar/StatusBar here
IMPORTANT: Root layout.tsx must NOT import NavBar/StatusBar anymore — those go in (app)/layout.tsx only

tasks.description: Create the login page with Google OAuth one-click button
steps: Create `ui-web/src/app/(auth)/auth/login/page.tsx` as a 'use client' component
Center the page content vertically and horizontally
Display: Siestai logo (Network icon in cyan square), 'Siestai' title, 'AI Agent Platform' subtitle, a styled 'Sign in with Google' button
On button click, call `signIn.social({ provider: 'google', callbackURL: '/' })` from auth-client
Use `useSession()` — if session exists and is not loading, redirect to '/' via `useRouter().replace('/')`
Show a loading spinner while auth state is resolving
Match the existing dark theme: bg-background, cyan primary, card-style container

tasks.description: Create Next.js middleware to redirect unauthenticated users to login page
steps: Create `ui-web/src/middleware.ts`
In the middleware function, forward the cookie header from the incoming request to `${NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL}/api/auth/get-session` via fetch with `credentials: 'include'`
If the response indicates no valid session (empty/error), return `NextResponse.redirect('/auth/login')`
If valid session, return `NextResponse.next()`
Configure matcher to exclude auth routes and static assets: `['/((?!auth|_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|icon.svg|api).*)']`
Handle fetch errors gracefully — if backend is unreachable, redirect to login (not a 500 error page)

tasks.description: Add user avatar dropdown and sign-out button to the NavBar
steps: In `ui-web/src/components/layout/nav-bar.tsx`, import `useSession` and `signOut` from `@/lib/auth-client`

Replace the standalone Settings gear icon with a user section containing: user avatar (from `session.data?.user.image` or initials fallback in a cyan circle) and a dropdown or inline links for Profile (/profile), Settings (/settings), and Sign Out
Sign Out calls `signOut()` then `router.push('/auth/login')`
On mobile menu: add user name/email at top, Sign Out at bottom
Import `useRouter` from `next/navigation` for redirect after sign out

tasks.description: Create the profile page where users can view and edit their display name
steps: Create `ui-web/src/app/(app)/profile/page.tsx` as a 'use client' component
Use `useSession()` to get current user data (name, email, image)
Display: user avatar (large), name (editable Input), email (read-only, greyed out — from Google), 'Member since' date
On save, call `authClient.updateUser({ name: newName })` from better-auth/react client
Show success/error toast or inline feedback
Reuse the SettingsSection/SettingsRow pattern from the settings page for consistent styling — either import them or duplicate the pattern
Add a link to /profile in the NavBar user dropdown (from task 12)

tasks.description: Update env examples and AGENTS.md documentation for the new auth system
steps: Update `backend/.env.example` with: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET (min 32 chars), BETTER_AUTH_URL=http://localhost:4200
Verify `ui-web/.env.example` has NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (already present — no changes expected)

Update `AGENTS.md` with an Auth section covering: Better Auth + Google OAuth setup, @thallesp/nestjs-better-auth integration, database tables (user/session/account/verification), user_id FK on agents, Google Cloud Console callback URL format, env vars needed
tasks.verify: grep GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID backend/.env.example && grep BETTER_AUTH_SECRET backend/.env.example

tasks.description: E2E: Verify login page renders and middleware redirects unauthenticated users
steps: agent-browser open http://localhost:3000 && agent-browser wait --load networkidle
Verify redirect to /auth/login (middleware should redirect unauthenticated users)
agent-browser screenshot --full screenshots/0005-google-oauth/login-page.png
agent-browser snapshot -i to verify Google sign-in button is present
agent-browser close
